200	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue}
201	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:modules_pullbackComp,
202	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
203	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}), with index set \(J\), charts \(U_i\),
204	  pairwise overlaps \(V_{ij}\), open immersions
205	  \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow U_i\), and transition isomorphisms
206	  \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\) satisfying the glue datum's
207	  cocycle conditions; write \(X\) for the glued scheme and
208	  \(\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\) for the canonical open immersions of the
209	  charts. Suppose given:
210	  \begin{itemize}
211	    \item for each \(i \in J\), a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) on the chart \(U_i\);
212	    \item for each pair \((i,j)\), a \emph{transition isomorphism} of sheaves of
213	      modules on the overlap \(V_{ij}\),
214	      \[
215	        g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
216	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
217	        (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j,
218	      \]
219	      comparing the two charts' sheaves after restriction (pullback) to the
220	      common overlap \(V_{ij}\);
221	  \end{itemize}
222	  \emph{subject to the following module cocycle conditions}, compatible with the
223	  scheme-level cocycle of \(D\) (its triple transition data \(t_{ij}\) and the
224	  overlap immersions \(f_{ij}\)), which are required as hypotheses on the family
225	  \((g_{ij})\):
226	  \begin{enumerate}
227	    \item[(C1)] \emph{self-identity:} the self-transition is the identity,
228	      \(g_{ii} = \mathrm{id}\) on \(f_{ii}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) (with
229	      \(V_{ii}\) and \(t_{ii}\) the diagonal data of \(D\));
230	    \item[(C2)] \emph{triple-overlap multiplicativity:} over each common triple
231	      overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\) the base-change transports
232	      (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three transition
233	      isomorphisms satisfy
234	      \[
235	        \widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
236	          \;=\; \widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j},
237	      \]
238	      where \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\), \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\),
239	      \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are the transports of \(g_{ij}\), \(g_{jk}\),
240	      \(g_{ik}\) to \(V_{ijk}\) through the scheme-level triple transition
241	      \(t'_{ijk}\) and its compatibility \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\), reassociated by the
242	      pullback comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} so that all three land on
243	      the same sheaf -- the multiplicative, \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued cocycle
244	      condition.
245	  \end{enumerate}
246	  Conditions (C1) and (C2) are exactly the descent datum for the Zariski cover
247	  \(\{\iota_i\}\); it is subject to them that the gluing is well-defined. Out of this
248	  data the construction produces a glued sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
249	  \(\mathcal{M}\) on the glued scheme \(X\).
250	
251	  \emph{Construction.} The category \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of sheaves of
252	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has all limits, and both pushforward along a morphism and
253	  these limits preserve the sheaf condition; the glued sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is therefore
254	  built directly as a limit -- an \emph{equalizer of pushforwards} -- rather than through a
255	  hand-built presheaf of compatible families. Concretely, form the two parallel maps of
256	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
257	  \[
258	    \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
259	      \;\;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\;\;
260	    \prod_{i,j} (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr),
261	  \]
262	  where, on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) pushes the \(i\)-th factor forward along the
263	  overlap immersion \(f_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction
264	  together with the pushforward-composition comparison
265	  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\circ f_{ij}^{*}\) (the dual of
266	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), and leg \(b\) pushes the \(j\)-th factor forward and
267	  additionally transports it across the inverse transition isomorphism \((g_{ij})^{-1}\)
268	  and the glue datum's compatibility identity, so that both legs land on the common overlap
269	  sheaf \((\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i)\). The glued sheaf is the
270	  equalizer
271	  \[
272	    \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a,b) \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
273	  \]
274	  the sub-sheaf of tuples of pushed-forward chart sections whose two overlap restrictions
275	  agree -- precisely the compatible families, now realised as a categorical limit rather than
276	  a bespoke presheaf. The cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on the family \((g_{ij})\) are
277	  \emph{not} consumed in forming this equalizer object: the limit exists for any family of
278	  transition maps. They are instead what make the equalizer descend correctly, and are
279	  exactly the hypotheses pinned down downstream when the restriction isomorphisms
280	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso} are produced. This specialises effective descent
281	  of sheaves of modules to the concrete open cover \(\{\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\}\)
282	  supplied by a scheme glue datum, and is the form in which the Grassmannian's
283	  tautological bundle and quotient are built.
284	
285	  The glued sheaf comes with three further structures, each recorded as its own block
286	  downstream: the \emph{restriction isomorphisms} \(\rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}
287	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\) compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps
288	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}; the \emph{characterisation up to unique
289	  isomorphism} by the restriction property \cref{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}; and the
290	  \emph{descent of morphisms} -- a family \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
291	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms glues to a unique \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to
292	  \mathcal{N}\) with \(\rho_i^{\mathcal{N}} \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi = \phi_i \circ
293	  \rho_i^{\mathcal{M}}\) \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}.
294	\end{definition}
295	
296	\begin{definition}[Restriction isomorphisms of the glued sheaf]
297	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}
298	  % NOTE: the Lean decl `glueRestrictionIso` is pinned canonically by
299	  % `def:glueRestrictionIso` in Picard_GlueDescent.tex (its 1:1 home after the
300	  % iter-067 file split). This block is the downstream consumer's cross-reference
301	  % node; it carries no \lean{} pin to avoid a duplicate.
302	  \uses{def:glueRestrictionIso, def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
303	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
304	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. For each
305	  chart index \(i\) there is a \emph{restriction isomorphism} of sheaves of
306	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules
307	  \[
308	    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,
309	  \]
310	  compatible with the transition isomorphisms: over each overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two
311	  restrictions \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\), transported to the common overlap, differ
312	  exactly by \(g_{ij}\).
313	\end{definition}
314	\begin{proof}
315	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
316	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
317	  By the equalizer-of-pushforwards description \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, a section of
318	  \(\mathcal{M}\) over an open of the form \(\iota_i^{-1}V\), restricted to the chart
319	  \(U_i\), is recovered from and recovers its \(i\)-th component
320	  \(s_i \in \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \cdot)\) -- the \(i\)-th factor of the product
321	  \(\prod_i (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) into which the equalizer embeds; the
322	  chart-restriction bridge \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso} identifies the pullback
323	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) with this restriction, and the \(i\)-th projection
324	  \((s_\bullet) \mapsto s_i\) is the isomorphism \(\rho_i\) (with inverse extending a chart
325	  section by the overlap relation).
326	  The overlap compatibility is the defining relation of the compatible families: over
327	  \(V_{ij}\) the components \(s_i, s_j\) are related by \(g_{ij}\), which is precisely the
328	  statement that \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\) differ by \(g_{ij}\) after transport to
329	  \(V_{ij}\); the triple-overlap coherence needed for this to be consistent is (C2),
330	  whose transports are aligned by \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} and
331	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}.
332	\end{proof}

334	\begin{lemma}[Uniqueness of the glued sheaf up to canonical isomorphism]
335	  \label{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}
336	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue_unique}
337	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
338	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
339	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
340	  Suppose \(\mathcal{M}'\) is another sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules equipped with
341	  isomorphisms \(\rho'_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}' \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\)
342	  compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps. Then there is a unique isomorphism
343	  \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) intertwining the restriction
344	  isomorphisms, i.e.\ with \(\rho'_i \circ \iota_i^{*}(\cdot) = \rho_i\) for every \(i\).
345	\end{lemma}
346	\begin{proof}
347	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
348	  A morphism of sheaves of modules on \(X\) is determined by, and may be assembled from,
349	  its restrictions to the members of the open cover \(\{\iota_i\}\), agreeing on the
350	  overlaps -- the locality and gluing of sections
351	  (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom. The
352	  intertwining condition forces the restriction over \(U_i\) to be
353	  \((\rho'_i)^{-1} \circ \rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}'\);
354	  these local isomorphisms agree on each overlap \(V_{ij}\) because both
355	  \((\rho_i)\) and \((\rho'_i)\) are compatible with the \emph{same} transition family
356	  \((g_{ij})\) (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}). By locality they glue to a
357	  unique global isomorphism \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) with the
358	  required property; uniqueness is the separation half of the same gluing.
359	\end{proof}

361	\begin{definition}[Descent of morphisms along the cover]
362	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
363	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueHom}
364	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
365	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
366	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
367	  Let \((\mathcal{M}_i, g_{ij})\) and \((\mathcal{N}_i, h_{ij})\) be two glued data
368	  over the same glue datum \(D\), with glued sheaves \(\mathcal{M}, \mathcal{N}\) and
369	  restriction isomorphisms \(\rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i, \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i\). Given a family
370	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-linear morphisms \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
371	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms on every overlap (i.e.\ \(h_{ij}\) intertwines
372	  the pullbacks of \(\phi_i\) and \(\phi_j\) over \(V_{ij}\)), there is a unique morphism
373	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) with
374	  \[
375	    \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi \;=\; \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i
376	    \qquad \text{for every } i.
377	  \]
378	\end{definition}
379	\begin{proof}
380	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
381	  On each chart the prescribed identity defines a local morphism
382	  \((\rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i)^{-1} \circ \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i :
383	  \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{N}\). The commutation of the \(\phi_i\)
384	  with the transition isomorphisms \((g_{ij}, h_{ij})\), together with the overlap
385	  compatibility of the \(\rho\)-families (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}), makes
386	  these local morphisms agree on every overlap \(V_{ij}\). By the locality and gluing of
387	  sections (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom they
388	  promote to a unique global morphism \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) restricting
389	  to each \(\phi_i\) through the \(\rho_i\) -- the descent of \(\mathrm{Hom}\) along the
390	  cover.
391	\end{proof}

393	\subsection{Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary morphism}
394	\label{sec:grquot_pullback_free}
395	
396	The functor of points (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}) acts on morphisms by pullback, so
397	it needs the pullback of the trivial bundle to be again trivial --
398	\(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}\) -- for an
399	\emph{arbitrary} scheme morphism \(\varphi\). Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison applies
400	only when the underlying site functor \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is \emph{final}; the
401	following three blocks remove that restriction for every morphism, and the resulting
402	pullback machinery preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness.
403	
404	\begin{lemma}\leanok
405	[The opens-pullback functor is final]
406	  \label{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
407	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final}
408	  For any morphism of schemes \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the inverse-image functor on opens
409	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi : \mathrm{Opens}(T) \to \mathrm{Opens}(T')\),
410	  \(U \mapsto \varphi^{-1}U\), is \emph{final}.
411	\end{lemma}
412	
413	\begin{proof}
414	
415	
416	\leanok
417	  Finality means that for every open \(V \subseteq T'\) the structured-arrow category of
418	  opens \(U \subseteq T\) with \(V \le \varphi^{-1}U\) is connected. That category is
419	  nonempty and has the terminal object \(U = \top\) -- one always has
420	  \(V \le \varphi^{-1}\top = T'\), and every object admits a unique arrow to \(\top\) --
421	  and a category with a terminal object is connected.
422	\end{proof}
423	
424	\begin{lemma}\leanok
425	[Pullback of a free sheaf is free]
426	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
427	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso}
428	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
429	  For any scheme morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), there is a canonical
430	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{T'}\)-modules
431	  \[
432	    \varphi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\bigr)
433	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
434	    \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}.
435	  \]
436	\end{lemma}
437	
438	\begin{proof}
439	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
440	  \leanok
441	  Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison produces this isomorphism whenever the site functor
442	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is final; that finality is \cref{lem:gr_opensMap_final},
443	  which holds for every morphism, so the comparison applies unconditionally.
444	\end{proof}
445	
446	\begin{lemma}
447	
448	\leanok
449	[Free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism]
450	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
451	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
452	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
453	  Let \(\varphi, \psi : T' \to T\) be morphisms with \(\varphi = \psi\) and \(I\) an index
454	  type. The two free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(\varphi\) and
455	  \(\psi\) agree after transporting the differing pullback sources by the canonical
456	  \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\): the composite of that transport with
457	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)\) equals \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)\).
458	\end{lemma}
459	\begin{proof}
460	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
461	  \leanok
462	  Substituting the equality \(\varphi = \psi\) collapses the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) to the
463	  identity and the two comparisons coincide.
464	\end{proof}
465	
466	\begin{lemma}
467	
468	\leanok
469	[Iso-level free-pullback cancellation]
470	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
471	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
472	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
473	  For equal base morphisms \(\varphi = \psi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), the composite
474	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)^{-1}\) is the
475	  \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) transport between the (differing) pullback sources of the free sheaf
476	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\). Stated and proved generically in the base morphisms, so its
477	  use never forces evaluation of a concrete immersion; this is the load-bearing replacement
478	  for the cast chain in the self-identity \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}.
479	\end{lemma}
480	\begin{proof}
481	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
482	  \leanok
483	  Substituting \(\varphi = \psi\) turns the composite into
484	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)^{-1} =
485	  \mathrm{id}\), which is the \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) of the now-trivial source equality.
486	\end{proof}

713	`p.appTop a`. Its proof transposes the naturality square under the pullback-pushforward
714	adjunction to a `unit`-level identity, which holds by naturality of the comorphism `p.c`. -/
715	
716	/-- The reduced (transposed) form of the scalar-naturality atom: on the unit sheaf,
717	multiplication by `a` followed by the comorphism `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` equals the
718	comorphism followed by the pushforward of multiplication by `p.appTop a`. Project-local
719	helper for `scalarEnd_pullback`. -/

721	    scalarEnd a ≫ SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
722	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
723	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a)) := by
724	  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj
725	    (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).unitHomEquiv.injective
726	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
727	  -- Both `.val Y` are nested applications (no morphism composite) up to defeq, since
728	  -- `unitHomEquiv (f ≫ p) = sectionsMap p (unitHomEquiv f)` and `sectionsMap`/`unitHomEquiv`
729	  -- are `rfl`/`sectionsMk`-defined; rewrite into that form via `change`.
730	  change (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
731	        ((scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
732	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
733	            (scalarEnd ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a))).val.app Y
734	        ((SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
735	          (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))

737	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply, map_one]

739	  -- RHS reduces (defeq, the pushforward's `map`-application is `rfl` + `scalarEnd_val_app_one`)
740	  -- to `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)`; LHS rewrites to the same by
741	  -- naturality of the comorphism `(toRingCatSheafHom p).hom` at `(homOfLE le_top).op : op ⊤ ⟶ Y`.
742	  have hnat := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom
743	    ((Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p).hom.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op) a
744	  rw [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, CategoryTheory.comp_apply] at hnat
745	  rw [hnat]

747	  -- scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd (p.appTop a)` at `1` over the preimage open; both sides are
748	  -- then `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)` (the `homOfLE`s agree by
749	  -- proof irrelevance, the comorphism by `forget₂`-on-elements), so `scalarEnd_val_app_one` closes.
750	  exact (scalarEnd_val_app_one ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a)
751	    (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map p.base).obj (Opposite.unop Y)))).symm

753	/-- **ATOM: scalar endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback`).
754	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `a ∈ Γ(S,⊤)`, pulling back the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` is,
755	after the unit-pullback comparison `q = pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the
756	base-changed function `p.appTop a`:
757	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ q = q ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`.
758	Proved by transposing under the pullback-pushforward adjunction to

807	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
808	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)
809	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom ≫
810	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
811	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv := by
812	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final p
813	  -- Reduce to the naturality square (cancel the trailing `Q.inv`).
814	  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
815	  -- Check the two maps out of the coproduct `(pullback p).obj (free (Fin d))` agree on each
816	  -- free injection `(pullback p).map (ιFree i)` (the cofan of the preserved colimit).
817	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
818	    (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (Scheme.Modules.pullback p) _ _
819	      (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d))) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
820	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]
821	  -- `Q.hom` is, by construction of `pullbackFreeIso`, the Mathlib free-pullback comparison.
822	  have hQhom : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
823	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin d)).hom := rfl

826	  have key : ∀ k : Fin d,
827	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
828	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
829	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
830	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
831	    intro k
832	    rw [hQhom]
833	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
834	  -- LHS: `map (ιFree i) ≫ map (matrixEnd M)` collapses to `map (ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M)`,
835	  -- then `ιFree_matrixEnd` turns it into a row sum, distributed by additivity of the pullback.
836	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
837	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)),
838	    ← Functor.map_comp]

840	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd M i]
841	  erw [Functor.map_sum]
842	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
843	  -- RHS: cancel `map (ιFree i) ≫ Q.hom` into `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree i`, then expand.
844	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
845	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom,
846	    key i]
847	  erw [Category.assoc]
848	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) i]
849	  erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
850	  -- Match term by term: each entry reduces to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
851	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => ?_)
852	  erw [Functor.map_comp]
853	  rw [Category.assoc, key k]
854	  erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)]
855	  erw [Category.assoc]

951	noncomputable def projFree {X : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (p : Fin d) :

956	/-- The global section of `O_X` carried by an endomorphism of the unit sheaf of modules:

960	    (e : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :

1102	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin m)).hom :=
1103	      congrArg (fun z => (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map z ≫

1132	/-! ### The rectangular matrix calculus: composition, identity, injectivity
1133	
1134	Support for the covering proof `chartLocus_isOpenCover`: the fully rectangular
1135	composition law (generalising `matrixEndRect_comp`), the identity law, injectivity of the
1136	matrix presentation, and the column-restriction law against `freeMap`. -/
1137	
1138	/-- For a square matrix the square and rectangular matrix endomorphisms coincide
1139	(they are definitionally the same biproduct matrix). Project-local bridge. -/
1140	lemma matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
1141	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) : matrixEnd M = matrixEndRect M := rfl
1142	
1143	/-- `matrixEndRect` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
1144	lemma matrixEndRect_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
1145	    matrixEndRect (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ :=
1146	  (matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect _).symm.trans matrixEnd_one
1147	
1148	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms, fully rectangular sizes — the
1149	categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEndRect_comp_rect`. -/
1150	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp_rect₂ {S : Scheme.{0}} {n e d : ℕ}
1151	    (mA : Fin n → Fin e →
1152	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf))
1153	    (mB : Fin e → Fin d →
1154	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
1155	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin n => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
1156	        (g := fun _ : Fin e => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mA ≫ biproduct.matrix mB
1157	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mA i p ≫ mB p q) := by
1158	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
1159	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
1160	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
1161	
1162	/-- **Fully rectangular composition law**: `matrixEndRect A ≫ matrixEndRect B =
1163	matrixEndRect (B * A)` for `A : e × n`, `B : d × e` — the matrix product, with the order
1164	reversed by the contravariance of the column/component indexing exactly as in
1165	`matrixEndRect_comp`. Project-local. -/
1166	lemma matrixEndRect_comp_rect {S : Scheme.{0}} {n e d : ℕ}
1167	    (A : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin n) Γ(S, ⊤)) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
1168	    matrixEndRect A ≫ matrixEndRect B = matrixEndRect (B * A) := by
1169	  rw [matrixEndRect, matrixEndRect, matrixEndRect]
1170	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin n) (p : Fin e) => scalarEnd (A p i))
1171	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin e) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (B p i))
1172	      = biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin n) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd ((B * A) p i)) := by
1173	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp_rect₂]
1174	    congr 1
1175	    funext i q
1176	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
1177	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
1178	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
1179	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
1180	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin n) (p : Fin e) => scalarEnd (A p i))),
1181	    hcomp]
1182	
1183	/-- **`matrixEndRect` is injective**: the presenting matrix of a free-sheaf morphism is
1184	unique (entry extraction `ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree` + `unitEndSection_scalarEnd`).
1185	Project-local. -/
1186	lemma matrixEndRect_injective {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1187	    {M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)} (h : matrixEndRect M = matrixEndRect N) :
1188	    M = N := by
1189	  refine Matrix.ext (fun p j => ?_)
1190	  have h1 : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p
1191	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect N ≫ projFree p :=
1192	    congrArg (fun z => SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ z ≫ projFree p) h
1193	  have h2 : scalarEnd (M p j) = scalarEnd (N p j) :=
1194	    (ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree M j p).symm.trans
1195	      (h1.trans (ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree N j p))
1196	  exact (unitEndSection_scalarEnd (M p j)).symm.trans
1197	    ((congrArg unitEndSection h2).trans (unitEndSection_scalarEnd (N p j)))
1198	
1199	/-- **Column restriction against `freeMap`**: precomposing a rectangular matrix morphism
1200	with the index inclusion `freeMap g` is taking the `g`-column submatrix. Project-local. -/
1201	lemma freeMap_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r e : ℕ} (g : Fin e → Fin r)
1202	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
1203	    SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := S.ringCatSheaf) g ≫ matrixEndRect M
1204	      = matrixEndRect (M.submatrix id g) := by
1205	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin e)) _ _ (fun j => ?_)
1206	  simp only [SheafOfModules.freeCofan_inj]
1207	  refine (cancel_mono (biproduct.isoCoproduct
1208	    (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).inv).mp ?_
1209	  refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
1210	  simp only [Category.assoc]
1211	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ (SheafOfModules.freeMap g ≫ matrixEndRect M) ≫ projFree p
1212	    = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect (M.submatrix id g) ≫ projFree p
1213	  calc SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ (SheafOfModules.freeMap g ≫ matrixEndRect M) ≫ projFree p
1214	      = (SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ SheafOfModules.freeMap g) ≫
1215	          matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p := by
1216	        simp only [Category.assoc]
1217	    _ = SheafOfModules.ιFree (g j) ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p :=
1218	        congrArg (· ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p)
1219	          (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap (R := S.ringCatSheaf) g j)
1220	    _ = scalarEnd (M p (g j)) := ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree M (g j) p
1221	    _ = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect (M.submatrix id g) ≫ projFree p :=
1222	        (ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree (M.submatrix id g) j p).symm

1224	/-! ### Epimorphisms between free sheaves split over affines
1225	
1226	The Nakayama/covering step of `chartLocus_isOpenCover` needs pointwise (fibre-level)
1227	surjectivity of an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Over an affine base this needs no
1228	stalk theory at all: the tilde functor `ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules` is fully
1229	faithful and additive, identifies the free sheaf with the tilde of the free module
1230	(`tildeFinsupp`), epimorphisms of modules are surjections, and free modules are
1231	projective — so any epimorphism between free sheaves on `Spec R` is *split*, and its
1232	presenting matrix admits a right inverse over the global sections. -/
1233	
1234	/-- **Epimorphisms between free sheaves of modules on `Spec R` split**: the splitting is
1235	transported through the fully faithful `tilde.functor` from the projectivity of the free
1236	module `Fin d →₀ R`. Project-local. -/
1237	lemma exists_section_of_epi_free_spec {R : CommRingCat.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1238	    (ψ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
1239	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) [Epi ψ] :
1240	    ∃ Φ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
1241	        SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r),
1242	      Φ ≫ ψ = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
1243	  -- the tilde-conjugate of `ψ` and its module-level preimage under full faithfulness
1244	  let ψt : tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R)) ⟶ tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=
1245	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv
1246	  let g : ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R) :=
1247	    tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimage ψt
1248	  have hg : tilde.map g = ψt := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.map_preimage ψt
1249	  haveI hiso_r : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin r)).hom := inferInstance
1250	  haveI hiso_d : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin d)).inv := inferInstance
1251	  haveI hψt : Epi ψt := by
1252	    -- fully explicit: the `(Spec R).Modules` / `(Spec (.of ↥R)).Modules` defeq blocks
1253	    -- automatic instance search across the composite
1254	    haveI h1 : Epi (ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv) :=
1255	      @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ ψ ‹Epi ψ› _ (IsIso.epi_of_iso _)
1256	    exact @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom (IsIso.epi_of_iso _) _ h1
1257	  haveI hge : Epi g := by
1258	    refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
1259	    show Epi (tilde.map g)
1260	    rw [hg]
1261	    exact hψt
1262	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective g.hom := (ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective g).mp hge
1263	  -- a module-level section of `g` by projectivity of the free module
1264	  obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property g.hom LinearMap.id hsurj
1265	  refine ⟨(tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1266	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom, ?_⟩
1267	  have hcomp : ModuleCat.ofHom w ≫ g = 𝟙 (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=
1268	    ModuleCat.hom_ext (by
1269	      rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_id, ModuleCat.hom_ofHom]
1270	      exact hw)
1271	  have hstep : (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ = tilde.map g ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by
1272	    rw [hg]
1273	    simp only [ψt, Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
1274	  calc ((tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1275	        (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom) ≫ ψ
1276	      = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1277	        ((tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ) := by simp only [Category.assoc]
1278	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1279	        tilde.map g ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by rw [hstep]
1280	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w ≫ g) ≫
1281	        (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by rw [← tilde.map_comp_assoc]
1282	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ 𝟙 _ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by
1283	        rw [hcomp, tilde.map_id]
1284	    _ = 𝟙 _ := by rw [Category.id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id]
1285	
1286	/-- **Presenting matrices of epimorphisms admit right inverses over `Spec R`**: combine
1287	the splitting `exists_section_of_epi_free_spec` with the matrix presentation of the
1288	section and the rectangular composition law. Project-local. -/
1289	lemma exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec {R : CommRingCat.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1290	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(Spec R, ⊤)) (h : Epi (matrixEndRect M)) :
1291	    ∃ G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) Γ(Spec R, ⊤), M * G = 1 := by
1292	  haveI := h
1293	  obtain ⟨Φ, hΦ⟩ := exists_section_of_epi_free_spec (matrixEndRect M)
1294	  refine ⟨Matrix.of (fun q p => unitEndSection
1295	    (SheafOfModules.ιFree p ≫ Φ ≫ projFree q)), ?_⟩
1296	  have hpres : matrixEndRect (Matrix.of (fun q p => unitEndSection
1297	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree p ≫ Φ ≫ projFree q))) = Φ := matrixEndRect_unitEndSection Φ
1298	  apply matrixEndRect_injective
1299	  rw [← matrixEndRect_comp_rect, hpres, hΦ, matrixEndRect_one]
1300	
1301	/-- **Presenting matrices of epimorphisms admit right inverses over any affine scheme** —
1302	the `Spec` case transported along `S.isoSpec` through the free-pullback comparisons
1303	(`pullback_conj_matrixEndRect`). Project-local; the algebraic heart of the Nakayama
1304	covering step. -/
1305	lemma exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} [IsAffine S] {d r : ℕ}
1306	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) (h : Epi (matrixEndRect M)) :
1307	    ∃ G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤), M * G = 1 := by
1308	  -- transport to `Spec Γ(S, ⊤)` along the inverse of `isoSpec`
1309	  haveI h1 : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M)) :=
1310	    @CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv)
1311	      inferInstance _ _ _ h
1312	  haveI h2 : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin r)).inv ≫
1313	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M) ≫
1314	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin d)).hom) := by
1315	    haveI : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M) ≫
1316	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin d)).hom) := epi_comp _ _
1317	    exact epi_comp _ _
1318	  have h3 : Epi (matrixEndRect (M.map
1319	      ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv)))) := by
1320	    rw [← pullback_conj_matrixEndRect S.isoSpec.inv M]
1321	    exact h2
1322	  obtain ⟨G', hG'⟩ := exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec
1323	    (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))) h3
1324	  -- pull the right inverse back along the (iso) global-sections comorphism
1325	  have hcancel : Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom
1326	      = 𝟙 (Γ(S, ⊤)) := by
1327	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, S.isoSpec.hom_inv_id]
1328	    simp
1329	  refine ⟨G'.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)), ?_⟩
1330	  have hM : (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))).map
1331	      ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) = M := by
1332	    rw [Matrix.map_map]
1333	    refine Matrix.ext (fun p j => ?_)
1334	    have hpt := congrArg (fun (z : Γ(S, ⊤) ⟶ Γ(S, ⊤)) =>
1335	      (CommRingCat.Hom.hom z) (M p j)) hcancel
1336	    simpa using hpt
1337	  calc M * G'.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom))
1338	      = (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))).map
1339	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) *
1340	        G'.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) := by rw [hM]
1341	    _ = ((M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))) * G').map
1342	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) :=
1343	        Matrix.map_mul.symm
1344	    _ = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _).map
1345	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) := by rw [hG']
1346	    _ = 1 := Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)

1352	/-- **Transport of a matrix automorphism through `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`** — the
1353	reusable (a)→(c) bridge for the bundle cocycle (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_transport`). A
1354	transition isomorphism of the bundle-transition shape
1355	`pullbackFreeIso a ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso b).symm` (a `GL_d` matrix
1356	automorphism conjugated to the overlap pullbacks) transports along `p : W ⟶ V` to the same
1357	shape over `p ≫ a` / `p ≫ b`, with the matrix base-changed entrywise by the comorphism
1358	`p.appTop`. Combines the matrix-naturality atom `matrixEnd_pullback` with the free-pullback
1359	pseudofunctor coherence `Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp`. Project-local — this is the
1360	abstract core of the bundle cocycle transport, independent of the Grassmannian charts. -/
1361	lemma pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V)
1362	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{0}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj) {d : ℕ}
1363	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(V, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
1364	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b
1365	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d) ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM ≪≫
1366	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).symm)).hom
1367	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom ≫
1368	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
1369	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
1370	  simp only [Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom,
1371	    Iso.symm_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom]
1372	  -- Front coherence: the `pullbackComp` cast + the `a`-leg comparison assemble into the

1374	  have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
1375	          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom ≫
1376	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d)).hom ≫
1377	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom

1379	    erw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp a p (Fin d)]
1380	    simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom]

1384	  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv ≫

1389	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)
1390	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)) ≪≫
1391	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d) := by
1392	      apply Iso.ext
1393	      simpa using Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp b p (Fin d)
1394	    have hinv := congrArg Iso.inv hiso
1395	    simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv] at hinv

1397	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Iso.app_hom]
1398	    erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]

800	\begin{lemma}[The $I$-columns of the chart quotient are the identity]
801	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
802	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_ιFree}
803	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
804	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
805	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
806	  For each \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), the chart quotient \(u^I\)
807	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) carries the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th basis section of the
808	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) -- where \(\iota_I(k)\) is the \(k\)-th
809	  element of \(I\) in increasing order -- to the \(k\)-th basis section of
810	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\); that is, the \(I\)-indexed columns of \(u^I\) form the
811	  identity:
812	  \[
813	    e_{\iota_I(k)} \,\circ\, u^I \;=\; e_k.
814	  \]
815	\end{lemma}
816	
817	\begin{proof}
818	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
819	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
820	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
821	  By the biproduct presentation of \(u^I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
822	  composite \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I\) reads off the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th column of the
823	  component matrix, whose \(p\)-th entry is the scalar endomorphism
824	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)})\). The \(I\)-indexed columns of the
825	  universal matrix \(X^I\), read through the order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong
826	  I\), form the \(d \times d\) identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}),
827	  so \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\). Hence the diagonal entry is
828	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
829	  off-diagonal entries are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
830	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the resulting column is the \(k\)-th coordinate
831	  inclusion, giving \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I = e_k\).
832	\end{proof}

834	\begin{lemma}[The chart quotient is an epimorphism]
835	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}
836	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_epi}
837	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient}
838	  The chart quotient \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
839	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules.
840	\end{lemma}
841	
842	\begin{proof}
843	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
844	  We exhibit a section, so that \(u^I\) is a \emph{split} epimorphism and hence an
845	  epimorphism. Let \(\iota_I : \{1,\dots,d\} \hookrightarrow \{1,\dots,r\}\) be the
846	  inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, \(k \mapsto \iota_I(k)\), the
847	  \(k\)-th element of \(I\) in increasing order. It induces a morphism of free
848	  sheaves \(s_I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\), the
849	  coordinate inclusion sending the \(k\)-th basis section to the
850	  \(\iota_I(k)\)-th. We claim
851	  \[
852	    u^I \circ s_I \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}}.
853	  \]
854	  Both sides are morphisms out of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf, which is the coproduct
855	  of \(d\) copies of the unit module; by the universal property of that coproduct
856	  it suffices to check the equality on each coordinate, i.e.\ to compare component
857	  matrices. The \((p,k)\)-component of \(u^I \circ s_I\) is the entry
858	  \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)}\) of the universal matrix at the \(I\)-indexed column, which
859	  is precisely the \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\). Since that minor is the
860	  \(d \times d\) identity (the columns of \(X^I\) indexed by \(I\), read through the
861	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong I\), form the identity block --
862	  \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), one has \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\),
863	  so \(u^I \circ s_I\) has the identity component matrix and equals
864	  \(\mathrm{id}\). Hence \(s_I\) splits \(u^I\) and \(u^I\) is a split
865	  epimorphism, in particular an epimorphism.
866	\end{proof}

868	\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
869	\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}
870	
871	The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
872	obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
873	rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
874	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
875	requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
876	it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
877	\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
878	\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
879	its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
880	package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
881	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
882	universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.
883

885	
886	[Self-minor Cramer inverse is the identity]
887	  \label{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_self}

889	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
890	  The Cramer inverse of the self-minor is the identity matrix:
891	  \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d \times d}\).
892	\end{lemma}
893	\begin{proof}
894	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
895	  The self-minor \(X^I_I\) is the identity block
896	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), and \(1^{-1} = 1\).
897	\end{proof}

899	\begin{definition}[The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\)]
900	  \label{def:gr_bundleTransition}
901	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition}
902	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
903	    lem:gr_minorDet_unit, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition}
904	  Let \(I, J \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) be size-\(d\) subsets and let
905	  \(f_{IJ} : U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\) and \(t_{IJ} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\) be
906	  the overlap immersion and transition of the chart cover (\cref{def:gr_transition}). The
907	  \emph{bundle transition} \(g_{I,J}\) is the isomorphism of sheaves of modules on the
908	  overlap \(U^I_J\)
909	  \[
910	    g_{I,J} \;:\;
911	      f_{IJ}^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\bigr)
912	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
913	      (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^J}^{\,d}\bigr)
914	  \]
915	  induced by the invertible \(d \times d\) matrix
916	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J)\) (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
917	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor, which is invertible because
918	  \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}).
919	
920	  \emph{Realisation.} The transition is built exactly as the chart quotient
921	  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}: each entry \((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q} \in R^I_J\) is a global
922	  function on the overlap, its scalar endomorphism
923	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q}\bigr)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the
924	  \((p,q)\)-component, and the universal property of the rank-\(d\) biproduct assembles
925	  these into an automorphism of the free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); the
926	  source and target pullbacks of free sheaves are identified with this
927	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\) through the free-pullback comparisons
928	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(f_{IJ}\) and \(t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI}\), conjugating
929	  the matrix automorphism into the displayed isomorphism. Invertibility of the matrix makes
930	  \(g_{I,J}\) an isomorphism.
931	\end{definition}

933	\begin{lemma}[Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)]
934	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}
935	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_self}
936	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
937	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
938	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
939	  The self-transition is the identity: \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\) on
940	  \(f_{II}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\). This is the module-level form of the descent
941	  condition (C1) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\).
942	\end{lemma}

944	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
945	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
946	  The \(I\)-th minor of the universal matrix is the identity block, \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
947	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), so its Cramer inverse is again the
948	  identity, \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}).
949	  Hence in the biproduct presentation of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} the diagonal
950	  components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the

952	  the assembled automorphism is the identity of \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_I}^{\,d}\); conjugation by

969	\begin{lemma}[Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap]
970	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}
971	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix}
972	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
973	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
974	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
975	  Over the common triple-overlap ring (the localisation carrying the three charts'
976	  data to \(V_{IJK}\)) the Cramer inverses of the localised minors satisfy the
977	  multiplicative cocycle identity
978	  \[
979	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}.
980	  \]
981	  This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent of any sheaf data.
982	\end{lemma}
983	\begin{proof}
984	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
985	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
986	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
987	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
988	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
989	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
990	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
991	  \[
992	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
993	  \]
994	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
995	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
996	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
997	  \[
998	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
999	  \]
1000	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
1001	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
1002	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
1003	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
1004	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
1005	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
1006	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
1007	\end{proof}

1009	\begin{lemma}[Scalar endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1010	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}
1011	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_pullback}
1012	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1013	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(a \in \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\) a
1014	  global function, with comorphism on global sections
1015	  \(p^{\sharp} : \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S) \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\).
1016	  Write \(q : p^{*}\mathbf{1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathbf{1}\) for the unit-pullback comparison
1017	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index). Then the pullback of the scalar endomorphism
1018	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is conjugate, through \(q\), to the scalar endomorphism of the
1019	  base-changed function:
1020	  \[
1021	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\bigr)
1022	      \;=\; q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(p^{\sharp} a\bigr) \,\circ\, q
1023	  \]
1024	  as endomorphisms of \(p^{*}\mathbf{1}\); equivalently
1025	  \(q \circ p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp} a) \circ q\). This is the
1026	  single-entry naturality atom underlying the matrix-level statement
1027	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}.
1028	\end{lemma}
1029	\begin{proof}
1030	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1031	  The scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is multiplication
1032	  by the global section \(a\) on the unit module \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}_S\). Pulling back
1033	  along \(p\) sends multiplication by \(a\) to multiplication by its image, and the comorphism
1034	  \(p^{\sharp}\) is precisely the action of pullback on global functions, so
1035	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a))\) is multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\). The
1036	  comparison \(q\) is an isomorphism of unit modules intertwining this multiplication on
1037	  \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\) with multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(\mathbf 1\); conjugating back
1038	  along \(q\) therefore yields the claimed identity. The verification is a single computation on
1039	  the unit module, where multiplication by a function commutes with the natural comparison
1040	  \(q\).
1041	\end{proof}

1043	\begin{lemma}[Matrix endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1044	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1045	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_pullback}
1046	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1047	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1048	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1049	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\). Let
1050	  \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T))\) be the entrywise
1051	  image of \(M\) under the comorphism \(p^{\sharp}\), and let
1052	  \(Q : p^{*}(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) be the free-pullback
1053	  comparison \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at the rank-\(d\) index set. Then the
1054	  pullback of the matrix endomorphism \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q\), to
1055	  the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1056	  \[
1057	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\bigr)
1058	      \;=\; Q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q .
1059	  \]
1060	\end{lemma}
1061	\begin{proof}
1062	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1063	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1064	  By \cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the biproduct-assembled endomorphism
1065	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} = \bigoplus_{k} \mathbf 1\) whose \((k,\ell)\)-component is
1066	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\). The pullback functor \(p^{*}\) is a left adjoint, hence
1067	  additive and biproduct-preserving; through the coproduct comparison underlying the
1068	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation
1069	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) and the assembled morphism to the
1070	  morphism reassembled from the pulled-back components, the reassociation of the iterated
1071	  pullbacks being \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the
1072	  pulled-back scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the
1073	  comparison-conjugate of \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp}M_{k,\ell}) =
1074	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the biproduct, the conjugating
1075	  one-element comparisons combine into the single free comparison \(Q\), giving
1076	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)) = Q^{-1}\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q\).
1077	\end{proof}

1409	The three scheme-pullback base-change maps `Γ(U^I_J,⊤) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK,⊤)` — induced by the two
1410	pullback projections and the triple transition `t'` — are identified, through the affine
1411	global-sections isomorphism `ΓSpecIso` and the away-pullback identification
1412	`V_IJK ≅ Spec S_I` (`awayPullbackIso`), with the ring homomorphisms `awayInclLeft`,
1413	`awayInclRight` and `cocycleΘIJ ∘ awayInclRight` over which the matrix cocycle L1
1414	(`bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix`) is stated. -/

1416	/-- **Affine global-sections comorphism is the inducing ring homomorphism**
1417	(`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec`): for a ring homomorphism `φ : A ⟶ B`, the
1418	global-sections comorphism of `Spec.map φ`, conjugated through the counit isomorphisms
1419	`ΓSpecIso`, is `φ` itself. Pure `Γ ⊣ Spec` naturality; project-local packaging. -/
1420	lemma baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec {A B : CommRingCat.{0}} (φ : A ⟶ B) :
1421	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop (Spec.map φ)
1422	      = φ ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso B).inv := by
1423	  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, Category.assoc,
1424	    Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]

1426	/-- The global-sections identification of the triple overlap: the ring map from the
1427	triple-overlap coordinate ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]` to the global sections of the
1428	scheme-level triple overlap `V_IJK = U^I_J ×_{U^I} U^I_K`, namely the affine identification
1429	`ΓSpecIso` transported through the away-pullback identification `awayPullbackIso`. It is the
1430	common codomain conjugation of the three base-change bridges below. Project-local. -/
1431	noncomputable def tripleOverlapSections (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1432	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1433	    CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) ⟶
1434	      Γ(Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK), ⊤) :=
1435	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
1436	    Scheme.Hom.appTop
1437	      (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom

1439	/-- **First-projection bridge to `awayInclLeft`** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left`): the
1440	global-sections base-change map of the first projection `p^{IJ}_{IJK} : V_IJK ⟶ U^I_J`,
1441	transported through the affine identifications, is the ring homomorphism
1442	`awayInclLeft (P^I_J) (P^I_K)`. Project-local. -/
1443	lemma baseChange_bridge_left (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1444	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1445	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv ≫
1446	        Scheme.Hom.appTop
1447	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))
1448	          (Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK))
1449	      = CommRingCat.ofHom (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) ≫
1450	        tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK := by
1451	  have hfst : (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).inv ≫
1452	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
1453	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1454	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
1455	    awayPullbackIso_inv_fst _ _
1456	  have hp : Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
1457	      = (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
1458	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1459	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
1460	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mp hfst
1461	  rw [hp, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
1462	  -- term-mode reassociation (positional `rw [← Category.assoc]` misses the comp node: the
1463	  -- middle-object representation differs across the `pullback (chartIncl …)` defeq)
1464	  exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans ((congrArg
1465	    (· ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop
1466	      (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom)
1467	    (baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec (CommRingCat.ofHom
1468	      (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))))).trans
1469	    (Category.assoc _ _ _))

1471	/-- **Second-projection bridge to `awayInclRight`** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_right`): the

1473	transported through the affine identifications, is the ring homomorphism
1474	`awayInclRight (P^I_J) (P^I_K)`. Project-local. -/
1475	lemma baseChange_bridge_right (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)

1477	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I K hI hK)))).inv ≫
1478	        Scheme.Hom.appTop
1479	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I K hI hK))))
1480	          (Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK))

1482	        tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK := by
1483	  have hsnd : (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).inv ≫
1484	        Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
1485	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1486	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
1487	    awayPullbackIso_inv_snd _ _
1488	  have hp : Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
1489	      = (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫

1491	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
1492	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mp hsnd
1493	  rw [hp, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
1494	  -- term-mode reassociation (see `baseChange_bridge_left`)

1496	    (· ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop
1497	      (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom)
1498	    (baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec (CommRingCat.ofHom
1499	      (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))))).trans

1502	/-- **Triple-transition bridge to `Θ_{IJ}`** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_transition`): the
1503	global-sections base-change map of the composite `t'_{IJK} ≫ p^{JK}_{JKI} : V_IJK ⟶ U^J_K`,
1504	transported through the affine identifications, is the localised cocycle homomorphism
1505	`Θ_{IJ} ∘ awayInclRight (P^J_I) (P^J_K)` — exactly the composite over which the matrix
1506	cocycle L1 (`bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix`) takes the `(J,K)`-Cramer inverse. The
1507	order-swap `awayMulCommEquiv` of `chartTransition'` is absorbed by
1508	`awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft`. Project-local. -/
1509	lemma baseChange_bridge_transition (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1510	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1511	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).inv ≫
1512	        Scheme.Hom.appTop
1513	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))))
1514	          (chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
1515	            Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI))
1516	      = CommRingCat.ofHom ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1517	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) ≫
1518	        tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK := by
1519	  have hfst : (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).inv ≫
1520	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)
1521	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1522	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI))) :=
1523	    awayPullbackIso_inv_fst _ _
1524	  have hp : chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
1525	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)
1526	      = (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
1527	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1528	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))) := by
1529	    rw [chartTransition']
1530	    simp only [Category.assoc]
1531	    -- `erw` (defeq matching) to fire the fst-leg lemma through the `HasPullback` instance
1532	    -- diamond on the heavy localisation objects (the Cells `chartTransition'_fac` precedent)
1533	    erw [hfst]
1534	    -- collapse the three `Spec.map`s in a fresh homogeneous `have` (positional `rw` cannot
1535	    -- see the `Spec.map ≫ Spec.map` nodes after the erw), then transport by `congrArg`
1536	    have htail : Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
1537	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1538	            (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
1539	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1540	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)))
1541	        = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1542	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))) := by
1543	      rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
1544	        ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft]
1545	    exact congrArg
1546	      ((awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫ ·) htail
1547	  -- `rw [hp]` cannot find the composite under `appTop` (comp-node instance mismatch);
1548	  -- transport by `congrArg` instead, then proceed as in `baseChange_bridge_left`.
1549	  refine (congrArg (fun m => (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1550	    (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop m) hp).trans ?_
1551	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
1552	  -- term-mode reassociation (see `baseChange_bridge_left`)
1553	  exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans ((congrArg
1554	    (· ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop
1555	      (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom)
1556	    (baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec (CommRingCat.ofHom ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1557	      (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))))).trans
1558	    (Category.assoc _ _ _))

1560	/-- **Base-change bridge to the localised cocycle, matrix form** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge`):
1561	over the triple overlap `V_IJK` the three geometrically base-changed Cramer inverses satisfy
1562	the multiplicative cocycle. The three projection bridges rewrite each base-changed matrix as
1563	the σ-image (`tripleOverlapSections`) of the corresponding L1 matrix, and the matrix-level
1564	cocycle `bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix` transports along the ring homomorphism σ.
1565	Project-local — this is the (b)-step of the bundle cocycle. -/
1566	theorem baseChange_bridge (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1567	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1568	    (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1569	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))))
1570	          (chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
1571	            Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)))).mapMatrix
1572	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1573	            (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK))
1574	      * (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1575	            (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))
1576	            (Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)
1577	              (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)))).mapMatrix
1578	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1579	              (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
1580	      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1581	            (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I K hI hK))))
1582	            (Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)
1583	              (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)))).mapMatrix
1584	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1585	              (minorDet d r I K hI hK)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
1586	            (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK)) := by
1587	  have hL := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (baseChange_bridge_left d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1588	  have hR := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (baseChange_bridge_right d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1589	  have hT := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (baseChange_bridge_transition d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1590	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom] at hL hR hT
1591	  -- collapse the iterated `mapMatrix`s into single `Matrix.map`s along composite ring homs,
1592	  -- rewrite the composites through the three bridges, and split off the σ-factor
1593	  simp only [RingHom.mapMatrix_apply, Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hL, hR, hT]
1594	  -- split off exactly the outer σ-layer of each factor, recombine the product under σ, and
1595	  -- close by the matrix cocycle L1; a `calc` keeps every sub-goal freshly elaborated (the
1596	  -- carrier representations `↥(of R)` vs `R` block positional `rw` on the simp-produced goal)
1597	  calc (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
1598	          ⇑((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)).comp
1599	            ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1600	              (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))) *
1601	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
1602	          ⇑((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)).comp
1603	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)))
1604	      = ((universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
1605	            ⇑((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1606	              (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).map
1607	            ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)) *
1608	        ((universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
1609	            ⇑(awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))).map
1610	            ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)) := by
1611	        simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Matrix.map_map]
1612	    _ = ((universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
1613	            ⇑((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1614	              (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) *
1615	          (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
1616	            ⇑(awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))).map
1617	            ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)) :=
1618	        Matrix.map_mul.symm
1619	    _ = ((universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK).map
1620	            ⇑(awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))).map
1621	            ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)) :=
1622	        congrArg (fun N => N.map
1623	            ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)))
1624	          (bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1625	    _ = (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK).map
1626	          ⇑((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)).comp
1627	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) := by
1628	        simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Matrix.map_map]

1630	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
1631	-- The endpoint-cast collapses rewrite under the `X.Modules` diamond on the heavy
1632	-- triple-overlap localisation objects; the raised limit covers the `isDefEq` cost
1633	-- (the Cells `chartTransition'_fac` precedent).
1634	/-- **Transport and endpoint alignment of the bundle transitions (the hom-level C2)**
1635	(`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport`): the underlying-morphism form of the triple-overlap
1636	multiplicativity. Each of the three base-change transports expands, via the abstract core
1637	`pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso`, into `Q ≫ matrixEnd(base-changed Cramer
1638	inverse) ≫ Q⁻¹`; the `pullbackCongr` endpoint casts collapse against the free-pullback
1639	comparisons (`pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr_hom` etc., all generic-`subst` lemmas), the two
1640	matrix endomorphisms fuse by `matrixEnd_comp`, and the resulting matrix identity is
1641	exactly the base-change bridge `baseChange_bridge` (b), i.e. the σ-image of the matrix
1642	cocycle L1. -/
1643	theorem bundleTransition_cocycle_transport (d r : ℕ) (I J K : (theGlueData d r).J) :
1644	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
1645	        (Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).f I K))
1646	        ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)
1647	        (bundleTransitionData d r I J)).hom ≫
1648	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
1649	          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app
1650	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))).hom ≫
1651	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
1652	        ((theGlueData d r).t' I J K ≫
1653	          Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f J K) ((theGlueData d r).f J I))
1654	        ((theGlueData d r).f J K) ((theGlueData d r).t J K ≫ (theGlueData d r).f K J)
1655	        (bundleTransitionData d r J K)).hom ≫
1656	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
1657	          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app
1658	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U K).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))).hom
1659	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
1660	          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app
1661	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))).hom ≫
1662	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
1663	        (Limits.pullback.snd ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).f I K))
1664	        ((theGlueData d r).f I K) ((theGlueData d r).t I K ≫ (theGlueData d r).f K I)
1665	        (bundleTransitionData d r I K)).hom := by
1666	  -- (1) expand the three transports via the abstract core (term-mode `have`s; the `g`-argument
1667	  -- `bundleTransitionData` is defeq to the `pullbackFreeIso ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso ≪≫ symm` shape)
1668	  have eIJ : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
1669	        (Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).f I K))
1670	        ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)
1671	        (bundleTransitionData d r I J)).hom
1672	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f I J)
1673	            ((theGlueData d r).f I K) ≫ (theGlueData d r).f I J) (Fin d)).hom ≫
1674	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1675	            (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2))))
1676	            (Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f I J)
1677	              ((theGlueData d r).f I K)))).mapMatrix
1678	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1679	              (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
1680	            (universalMinorInv d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2))) ≫
1681	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f I J)
1682	            ((theGlueData d r).f I K) ≫ ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I))
1683	            (Fin d)).inv :=
1684	    pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso _ _ _ _ _ _ _
1685	  have eJK : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport

1103	\begin{lemma}[First-projection bridge to \texorpdfstring{$\mathrm{awayInclLeft}$}{awayInclLeft}]
1104	  \label{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}
1105	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.baseChange_bridge_left}
1106	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_chart_incl, def:gr_away_pullback_iso,
1107	    lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst, def:gr_away_incl_left,
1108	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1109	  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) with triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\) of the glue
1110	  datum (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}), and let
1111	  \(p^{IJ}_{IJK} : V_{IJK} \to U^I_J\) be the first projection (the pullback projection of the
1112	  chart inclusion \(\mathrm{chartIncl}\), \cref{def:gr_chart_incl}). Through the away-pullback
1113	  identification \(V_{IJK} \cong \Spec R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]\) (\cref{def:gr_away_pullback_iso})
1114	  the projection \(p^{IJ}_{IJK}\) is the \(\Spec\) morphism of the left away-inclusion
1115	  \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}(P^I_J, P^I_K)\) (\cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst},
1116	  \cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}). Consequently the global-sections base-change map of
1117	  \(p^{IJ}_{IJK}\), transported through the affine global-sections identification, equals the
1118	  ring homomorphism \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}(P^I_J, P^I_K)\); in particular the entrywise image of
1119	  the Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) under the geometric base-change agrees with its image under
1120	  \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}\).
1121	\end{lemma}
1122	\begin{proof}
1123	  \uses{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst, def:gr_away_incl_left,
1124	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1125	  By \cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst} the first pullback projection, precomposed with the
1126	  away-pullback identification \cref{def:gr_away_pullback_iso}, equals
1127	  \(\Spec(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}(P^I_J, P^I_K))\). The source and overlap charts are affine, so by
1128	  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec} the global-sections comorphism of this \(\Spec\)
1129	  morphism is, through the affine global-sections identification, the ring homomorphism
1130	  \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}(P^I_J, P^I_K)\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}). Applying the comorphism
1131	  entrywise to \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) gives the asserted agreement.
1132	\end{proof}
